title: Depth queue by thermal sensing
abstract: A method (300) for characterizing a lighting environment using thermal imaging includes the steps of: providing (310) a lighting unit (10) comprising a light source (12), a thermal imager (32), and a controller (22); obtaining (330), using the thermal imager, one or more thermal images of one or more surfaces (52) within the lighting environment; extracting (340), by the controller using the one or more thermal images, a thermal shadow (54) on one or more surfaces within the lighting environment; determining (360), from the thermal shadow, a depth queue for an object (52) associated with the thermal shadow; and characterizing (370), by the controller using the determined depth queue, the object.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method (300) for determining a depth queue for an object using thermal imaging, the method comprising the steps of: obtaining (330), using a thermal imager, one or more thermal images of one or more surfaces (52) within an environment; extracting (340), by a controller using the one or more thermal images, a thermal signature (54) on one or more surfaces within the environment, wherein the thermal signature results from a thermal shadow created by a heat signal reflecting off an object exposed to a heat source, or by a thermally-active object with a higher temperature than the surrounding environment, radiating heat; determining (360), from the thermal signature, a depth queue for an object; and characterizing (370), by the controller using the determined depth queue, the object. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of extracting a thermal signature comprises comparing a thermal image at a first time point to a thermal image at a second time point. 3. A method (300) for characterizing a lighting environment using thermal imaging, the method comprising the steps of: providing (310) a lighting unit (10) comprising a light source (12), a thermal imager (32), and a controller (22); obtaining (330), using the thermal imager, one or more thermal images of one or more surfaces (52) within the lighting environment; extracting (340), by a controller using the one or more thermal images, a thermal signature (54) on one or more surfaces within the lighting environment, wherein the thermal signature results from a thermal shadow created by a heat signal reflecting off an object exposed to a heat source, or by a thermally- active object with a higher temperature than the surrounding environment, radiating heat; 

 determining (360), from the thermal signature, a depth queue for an object (52) associated with the thermal signature; and characterizing (370), by the controller using the determined depth queue, the object. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the step of extracting a thermal signature comprises comparing a thermal image at a first time point to a thermal image at a second time point. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the step of characterizing the object comprises identifying the object, determining a height of the object, determining an orientation of the object, or localizing the object within the lighting environment. 6. A lighting unit (10) configured to characterize a lighting environment using thermal imaging, the lighting unit comprising: a light source (12); a thermal imager (32) configured to obtain one or more thermal images of one or more surfaces (52) within the lighting environment; and a controller (22) configured to: (i) extract, using the one or more thermal images, a thermal shadow (54) on one or more surfaces within the lighting environment, wherein the thermal shadow results from a heat signal reflecting off an object exposed to a heat source, or from a thermally-active object with a higher temperature than the surrounding environment, radiating heat within the lighting environment; (ii) determine, from the thermal shadow, a depth queue for the object; and (iii) characterize the object using the determined depth queue. 7. The lighting unit of claim 6, further comprising a communications module (34) configured to communicate the extracted thermal shadow or the determined depth queue. 8. The lighting unit of claim 6, wherein the step of extracting a thermal shadow comprises comparing a thermal image at a first time point to a thermal image at a second time point. 9. A system (200) configured to characterize a lighting environment using thermal imaging, the system comprising: a plurality of lighting units (10), each of the plurality of lighting units comprising a light source (12); a thermal imager (32) configured to obtain one or more thermal images of one or more surfaces (52) within the lighting environment; and a communications module (34) configured to communicate the one or more thermal images; and a controller (22) configured to: (i) receive, from one or more of the communications modules, the one or more thermal images; (ii) extract, using the one or more thermal images, a thermal shadow (54) on one or more surfaces within the lighting environment, wherein the thermal shadow results from a heat signal reflecting off an object exposed to a heat source, or from a thermally- active object with a higher temperature than the surrounding environment, radiating heat within the lighting environment; (iii) determine, from the thermal shadow, a depth queue for the object; and (iv) characterize the object using the determined depth queue. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein extracting a thermal shadow comprises comparing, by the controller, a thermal image at a first time point to a thermal image at a second time point. 11. The system of claim 9, wherein the step of characterizing the object comprises identifying the object, determining a height of the object, determining an orientation of the object, or localizing the object within the lighting environment.
